ddls:
CREATE TABLE "hardware_components" (
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hardware component */,
  "component_name" TEXT /* Name of the hardware component (e.g., CPU, Memory, Storage) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the hardware component */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the manufacturer of the hardware component */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the hardware component */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the hardware component */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the hardware component was installed */,
  "warranty_expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the warranty for the hardware component expires */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the hardware component (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware component */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., Temperature, Voltage) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., Celsius, Volts) */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of the metric (e.g., temperature, voltage, usage) */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum acceptable value for the metric */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum acceptable value for the metric */,
  "alert_threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for generating alerts */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message to be displayed when an alert is generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metrics_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES hardware_components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance data point */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware component */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the metric */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the metric at a given timestamp */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data was collected */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the performance data (e.g., sensor, API, manual) */,
  "data_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the performance data (e.g., 99.5%) */,
  "data_reliability" TEXT /* Reliability of the performance data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the performance data (e.g., good, fair, poor) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES hardware_components ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dashboards" (
  "dashboard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dashboard */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who created the dashboard */,
  "dashboard_name" TEXT /* Name of the dashboard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dashboard */,
  "dashboard_type" TEXT /* Type of the dashboard (e.g., real-time, historical, custom) */,
  "dashboard_layout" TEXT /* Layout of the dashboard (e.g., grid, list) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp when the dashboard was last updated */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Whether the dashboard is public or private */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dashboard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dashboards_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "widgets" (
  "widget_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each widget */,
  "dashboard_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the dashboard */,
  "widget_name" TEXT /* Name of the widget */,
  "widget_type" TEXT /* Type of the widget (e.g., chart, table) */,
  "widget_size" TEXT /* Size of the widget (e.g., small, medium, large) */,
  "widget_position" TEXT /* Position of the widget on the dashboard (e.g., top-left, center) */,
  "data_refresh_interval" INTEGER /* Interval at which the widget data is refreshed (in seconds) */,
  "is_interactive" INTEGER /* Whether the widget is interactive (e.g., can be clicked, dragged) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("widget_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_widgets_dashboard_id FOREIGN KEY ("dashboard_id") REFERENCES dashboards ("dashboard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Whether the user has administrative privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permission" TEXT /* Permission assigned to the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_level" INTEGER /* Level of the role (e.g., 1 for admin, 2 for analyst, 3 for engineer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "permission_level" INTEGER /* Level of the permission (e.g., 1 for create, 2 for view, 3 for delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the hardware component */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the metric */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value that triggered the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved, ignored) */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, high, medium, low) */,
  "alert_resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "alert_resolved_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES hardware_components ("component_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_alert_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("alert_resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the activity */,
  "activity" TEXT /* Description of the activity */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the activity occurred */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of the log (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "log_level" INTEGER /* Level of the log (e.g., 1 for info, 2 for warning, 3 for error) */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log (e.g., user, system, external) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "setting_category" TEXT /* Category of the setting (e.g., system, security, performance) */,
  "setting_description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "setting_value_type" TEXT /* Type of the setting value (e.g., string, integer, boolean) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "integrations" (
  "integration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each integration */,
  "integration_name" TEXT /* Name of the integration */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* API key for the integration */,
  "integration_type" TEXT /* Type of the integration (e.g., API, webhook) */,
  "integration_status" TEXT /* Status of the integration (e.g., active, inactive, error) */,
  "last_synced" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last synchronization with the integrated system */,
  "sync_frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of synchronization (in minutes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("integration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_control" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the performance data */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the version was created */,
  "version_type" TEXT /* Type of the version (e.g., major, minor, patch) */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the version */,
  "version_created_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who created the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_control_version_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("version_created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_control_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES performance_data ("data_id")
)
